en: And he told them a parable to the effect that they ought always to pray and not lose heart.
zh: 耶穌對他們講一個比喻，論到人必須常常祈禱，不可灰心。

en: He said, "In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected man.
zh: 他說：“某城裡有一個法官，不懼怕 神，也不尊敬人。

en: And there was a widow in that city who kept coming to him and saying, 'Give me justice against my adversary.'
zh: 那城裡有一個寡婦，常常來到他那裡，說：‘求你給我伸冤，使我脫離我的對頭！’

en: For a while he refused, but afterward he said to himself, 'Though I neither fear God nor respect man,
zh: 他多次不肯，後來心裡說：‘我雖然不懼怕 神，也不尊敬人，

en: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will give her justice, so that she will not beat me down by her continual coming.'"
zh: 只是因為這寡婦常常來麻煩我，就給她伸冤吧，免得她不斷地來纏擾我。’”

en: And the Lord said, "Hear what the unrighteous judge says.
zh: 主說：“你們聽聽這不義的法官所說的話吧。

en: And will not God give justice to his elect, who cry to him day and night? Will he delay long over them?
zh: 難道 神不會為晝夜呼籲他的選民伸冤嗎？難道 神會耽誤他們嗎？

en: I tell you, he will give justice to them speedily. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?"
zh: 我告訴你們，他要快快地給他們伸冤。然而人子來的時候，在世上找得到這種信心嗎？”

en: He also told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and treated others with contempt:
zh: 耶穌向那些自恃為義、輕視別人的人，講了這個比喻：

en: "Two men went up into the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
zh: “有兩個人上聖殿去祈禱，一個是法利賽人，一個是稅吏。

en: The Pharisee, standing by himself, prayed thus: 'God, I thank you that I am not like other men, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
zh: 法利賽人站著，禱告給自己聽，這樣說：‘ 神啊，我感謝你，我不像別人，勒索、不義、姦淫，也不像這個稅吏。

en: I fast twice a week; I give tithes of all that I get.'
zh: 我一個禮拜禁食兩次，我的一切收入都奉獻十分之一。’

en: But the tax collector, standing far off, would not even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, 'God, be merciful to me, a sinner!'
zh: 稅吏卻遠遠站著，連舉目望天也不敢，只捶著胸說：‘ 神啊，可憐我這個罪人！’

en: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified, rather than the other. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted."
zh: 我告訴你們，這個人回家去，比那個倒算為義了。因為高抬自己的，必要降卑；自己謙卑的，必要升高。”

en: Now they were bringing even infants to him that he might touch them. And when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
zh: 有人抱著嬰孩到耶穌面前，要他觸摸他們。門徒看見了，就責備那些人。

en: But Jesus called them to him, saying, "Let the children come to me, and do not hinder them, for to such belongs the kingdom of God.
zh: 耶穌卻叫他們過來，說：“讓小孩子到我這裡來，不要禁止他們，因為 神的國正屬於這樣的人。

en: Truly, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child shall not enter it."
zh: 我實在告訴你們，凡不像小孩子一樣接受 神的國的，決不能進去。”

en: And a ruler asked him, "Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"
zh: 有一個官長問耶穌：“良善的老師，我當作甚麼，才可以承受永生？”

en: And Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.
zh: 耶穌對他說：“你為甚麼稱我是良善的呢？除了 神一位以外，沒有良善的。

en: You know the commandments: 'Do not commit adultery, Do not murder, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honor your father and mother.'"
zh: 誡命你是知道的：不可姦淫，不可殺人，不可偷盜，不可作假見證，當孝敬父母。”

en: And he said, "All these I have kept from my youth."
zh: 他說：“這一切我從小都遵守了。”

en: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, "One thing you still lack. Sell all that you have and distribute to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me."
zh: 耶穌聽了，就告訴他：“你還缺少一件，應當變賣你一切所有的，分給窮人，你就必定有財寶在天上，而且你還要來跟從我。”

en: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was extremely rich.
zh: 他聽見這話，非常憂愁，因為他十分富有。

en: Jesus, looking at him with sadness, said, "How difficult it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!
zh: 耶穌見他這樣，就說：“富有的人要進 神的國，是多麼難哪！

en: For it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."
zh: 駱駝穿過針眼，比有錢的人進 神的國還容易呢。”

en: Those who heard it said, "Then who can be saved?"
zh: 聽見的人說：“這樣，誰可以得救呢？”

en: But he said, "What is impossible with men is possible with God."
zh: 耶穌說：“人所不能的，在 神卻是可能的。”

en: And Peter said, "See, we have left our homes and followed you."
zh: 彼得說：“你看，我們已捨棄自己所有的，跟從你了！”

en: And he said to them, "Truly, I say to you, there is no one who has left house or wife or brothers or parents or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,
zh: 耶穌說：“我實在告訴你們，人為 神的國撇下房屋、妻子、弟兄、父母或兒女，

en: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the age to come eternal life."
zh: 沒有不在今世得許多倍，來世得永生的。”

en: And taking the twelve, he said to them, "See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written about the Son of Man by the prophets will be accomplished.
zh: 耶穌把十二門徒帶到一邊，對他們說：“我們現在上耶路撒冷去，先知所寫的一切，都要成就在人子身上。

en: For he will be delivered over to the Gentiles and will be mocked and shamefully treated and spit upon.
zh: 他要被交給外族人，受戲弄，被凌辱，他們要向他吐唾沫，

en: And after flogging him, they will kill him, and on the third day he will rise."
zh: 鞭打他，殺害他；第三日他要復活。”

en: But they understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what was said.
zh: 這些話的含義對門徒是隱藏的。他們聽了，一點也不明白，不知道他說的是甚麼。

en: As he drew near to Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.
zh: 耶穌走近耶利哥的時候，有一個瞎眼的人坐在路旁討飯，

en: And hearing a crowd going by, he inquired what this meant.
zh: 他聽見人群經過，就問是甚麼事。

en: They told him, "Jesus of Nazareth is passing by."
zh: 別人告訴他，是拿撒勒人耶穌經過。

en: And he cried out, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"
zh: 他就喊叫說：“大衛的子孫耶穌啊，可憐我吧！”

en: And those who were in front rebuked him, telling him to be silent. But he cried out all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
zh: 在前頭走的人責備他，叫他不要出聲；但他越發喊叫：“大衛的子孫，可憐我吧！”

en: And Jesus stopped and commanded him to be brought to him. And when he came near, he asked him,
zh: 耶穌站住，吩咐領他過來。等他走近，就問他：

en: "What do you want me to do for you?" He said, "Lord, let me recover my sight."
zh: “你要我為你作甚麼？”他說：“主啊，叫我能看見！”

en: And Jesus said to him, "Recover your sight; your faith has made you well."
zh: 耶穌說：“你就看見吧！你的信使你痊愈了。”

en: And immediately he recovered his sight and followed him, glorifying God. And all the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God.
zh: 他立刻看見了，就跟隨耶穌，頌讚 神。眾人看見這事，也讚美 神。
